title: opuntia galapageia subsp. insularis
intro: Opuntia galapageia subsp. insularis, also known as Galapagos prickly pear cactus, is a subspecies of Opuntia galapageia, a type of cactus native to the Galapagos Islands. This plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Opuntia galapageia subsp. insularis include its potential to treat various health conditions such as wounds, skin issues, and even certain types of cancer, due to its ability to stimulate collagen production and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into ointments, gels, or infusions, and are often used to soothe sunburns, reduce pain, and promote wound healing.
